title: [BUG] addRow() not adding merged cells correctly which are read from source file
author: aksharj

comments.author: aksharj
aksharj
comments.body:
Hi, any help on how to fix this will be really great. running out of ideas as to how to fix this. 
 I am ready to work on a PR as well can someone please point me in the correct direction?

Thanks.

comments.author: aksharj
comments.body:
If i try to merge cells by hardcoding, something like `myworksheet.mergeCells('B1:AC1');`, i get the error `Cannot merge already merged cells`. But the cells in the excel do not appear to be merged

```
Error: Cannot merge already merged cells
    at /node_modules/exceljs/lib/stream/xlsx/worksheet-writer.js:438:15
    at Array.forEach (<anonymous>)
    at WorksheetWriter.mergeCells (/node_modules/exceljs/lib/stream/xlsx/worksheet-writer.js:436:18)
    at WorksheetReader.<anonymous> (/lib/excel/transformer.js:55:27)
    at WorksheetReader.emit (events.js:310:20)
    at WorksheetReader.EventEmitter.emit (domain.js:482:12)
    at WorksheetReader.read (/node_modules/exceljs/lib/stream/xlsx/worksheet-reader.js:96:16)
    at processTicksAndRejections (internal/process/task_queues.js:97:5)
    at async WorkbookReader.read (/node_modules/exceljs/lib/stream/xlsx/workbook-reader.js:57:13)
```

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body: Probably I can't help you because of the lack of time to investigate it deeply. However my will you try to use non-stream API?

comments.author: aksharj
Siemienik
comments.body:
Probably, your problem follows from that, when `on('worksheet',...)` is called, then not all rows are read. That means. the worksheet object hasn't full knowledge about its model.

I'm happy that your code works :+1:

comments.author: eug-vs
MGber
comments.body:
Hello ExcelJS community,

I wanted to share a solution I implemented to handle the challenge of inserting rows in Excel files with merged cells. As many of you might know, managing merged cells during row insertions can be tricky, and I didn't find a built-in method in ExcelJS that handles this seamlessly.

Overview of the Solution:

1) Unmerge Cells: Initially, all merged cells are unmerged to simplify the insertion process.
2) Track Indices: The system keeps track of the indices of the originally merged cells to remember their initial state.
3) Handle Insertions/Deletions: When adding or removing rows, the indices are adjusted accordingly to maintain data consistency.
4) Duplicating Rows with Merged Cells: To duplicate a row with merged cells, simply insert a new row and transpose in the system the merges from the row to be duplicated to the new row.
5) Re-merge Cells: After the modifications are complete, cells are re-merged based on their original state using the tracked indices.

While I can't share the enterprise-specific code, I hope this high-level overview provides some guidance or inspiration for those facing a similar challenge. If anyone has further questions or would like more details on the logic, I'd be happy to discuss !

To access the merges in TypeScript was something like : 
`const merges = ((sheet.model) as any)._merges;`

Best regards, Maxime Gaber.

comments.author: tdr2d
comments.body:
Hello,
I faced this issue as well, I also wanted to add rows and keep the merging model at the same time.
Adding a new row also breaks the merging model of the rows underneath.
So, I ended up **not using merged cells, rather centering the data horizontally on multiple cells**: [https://kb.wisc.edu/helpdesk/page.php?id=119089](https://kb.wisc.edu/helpdesk/page.php?id=119089)

comments.author: imfurankase
CJxD
IonelLupu
comments.body:
I am still getting `Cannot merge already merged cells` even after using the `moveMergesModel` helper above. 😞  I even used [this approach](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/discussions/1967#discussioncomment-2172573) of unmerging the cells before merging them. Getting the same error. 

What can be done?

comments.author: dolphinxx
comments.body:
> I am still getting `Cannot merge already merged cells` even after using the `moveMergesModel` helper above. 😞 I even used [this approach](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/discussions/1967#discussioncomment-2172573) of unmerging the cells before merging them. Getting the same error.
> 
> What can be done?

Don't use `worksheet.mergeCells()`, because the `_merges` is not updated when inserting/deleting rows. Instead use `cell.merge(masterCell, ignoreStyle)` , which does not check the merge status via `_merges`.

comments.author: tdr2d
